Patch Set 6:

Shadow,
Both Nikitas and I had inspection comments on patch #5 that were ignored when you refreshed the patch. This has happened on several other of your patches as well. Please check for inspection comments in Gerrit when you are refreshing your patches. If you disagree with some inspection comment, then please provide a reply comment with why you disagree. Do not just ignore the inspection comments.

Patch Set 7:

I only know a little about selinux but:

I think there should be a xattr security.selinux at the inode size for selinux to work.  Faking the xattr seems to give a false sense of security.  

Why not allow the security.selinux xattr in the inode and support Selinux?

Patch Set 7:

Keith,

lustre have fake superblock to have start a lustre setup process, that SB don't have any real inodes and any other real resources but present in system and need to mount start. But Selinux don't able to mount a FS if they don't have an xattr functions - so that fake functions need just to start lustre stack only.

Patch Set 7: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Ok thanks for the clarification.
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as f6e931c58a2e4df5f330ae2e5969ba44398fa151

parents.subject: LU-3446 mdd: changelog index to survive MDT restart

commit.subject: LU-2655 obdclass: selinux support
commit.message:
LU-2655 obdclass: selinux support

add ability to start lustre targers on selinux enabled nodes
provide a fake xattr functions for a lustre mounts,
add security context to mkfs mountpoint.

Xyratex-bug-id: MPR-558
Signed-off-by: Alexey Lyashkov <alexey_lyashkov@xyratex.com>
Change-Id: Iee8701912e71da21eb7e2417ebdc123f88ab86fa
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/5135
Tested-by: Hudson
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Keith Mannthey <keith.mannthey@intel.com>
